meta.audit_limitations: Savage X Fenty requires active membership to access product detail pages and full search results. Findings F03, F04, F05 are partially based on Constructor.io API data (key captured via network inspection) rather than full visual browser observation. Findings F01, F02, F06 are fully confirmed via live browser screenshots. SAYT findings G01, G02 are fully confirmed.

cover.status_line: Confidential · For Internal Algolia Use Only

company_snapshot.industry: Lingerie & Intimate Apparel — DTC Subscription Commerce
company_snapshot.current_search_vendor: Constructor.io (ACTIVE since Nov 2023 — confirmed via 79 API calls + React component class names)

executives.name: Rihanna
executives.title: Founder & Executive Chair, Savage X Fenty
executives.quote: The whole idea of the retail space is that I wanted women and men to come in and feel represented, to walk away from the experience feeling confident and that they belong. I've experienced being a customer and shopping in the lingerie space and have felt as if I didn't fit the mold of the product. But Savage X Fenty is very inclusive, and I want to continue to expand on that so that our consumers can feel our commitment within the retail space.
executives.relevance: Inclusive sizing is Rihanna's core brand thesis — but search fails to surface products by size intent. 'Plus size bra' returns non-plus-size results. This contradicts the brand promise at the most critical discovery moment.

executives.name: Rihanna
executives.title: Founder & Executive Chair, Savage X Fenty
executives.quote: Having the 'Fit Xperience' is one of those things that makes it really important because people don't even know their size and we are shopping online.
executives.relevance: Explicit acknowledgment that online shoppers don't know their size — they need guided search, faceted navigation by body type, and AI-powered size recommendations. Algolia's attribute-aware search + Recommend API bridges this gap.

executives.name: Christiane Pendarvis and Natalie Guzman
executives.title: Co-Presidents, Savage X Fenty
executives.quote: Fit Xperience has been a game changer in merging the physical and digital retail brand, especially at a time when fit and comfort in your body is so important.
executives.relevance: 'Merging physical and digital' is the strategic mandate — Algolia federated search + mobile SDK delivers exactly this across web, app, and in-store kiosk.

executives.name: Christiane Pendarvis and Natalie Guzman
executives.title: Co-Presidents, Savage X Fenty
executives.quote: With this new iteration of Fit Xperience, we're excited to continue providing a seamless and positive shopping experience for everyBODY.
executives.relevance: 'everyBODY' = inclusive sizing promise. Delivering this online requires attribute-rich faceted search — size, cup, band, style, coverage — none of which is fully served by current Constructor.io static facets.

executives.name: Vaughn Harber
executives.title: VP Product Management, Savage X Fenty
executives.quote: By implementing Whitespectre's recommendations, we enhanced the customer journey, streamlined the path to purchase, and successfully brought our refreshed brand vision to life—ultimately delivering a measurable impact on engagement, conversion and our brand presence.
executives.relevance: 'Streamlined path to purchase' and 'measurable impact on conversion' are explicit KPIs. Algolia's benchmark: +37% conversion rate (Lacoste) — directly addressable via the same conversion-focused mandate.

executives.name: Alan Chang
executives.title: Product Management Director, Techstyle Operations Services
executives.quote: What keeps me up at night is that my product managers are blocked from being able to activate the data and make critical business decisions or run experiments.
executives.relevance: Experimentation is a core competency — they trust data enough to run A/B tests across every surface. Algolia's analytics dashboard gives the same rigor to search: query-level CTR, zero-result rates, and click position data feed directly into their existing experimentation culture.

intelligence_signals.text: Savage X Fenty raised $115M in February 2025 — the fourth institutional round totaling $310M+ — with stated plans to expand product range, enhance digital marketing efforts, bolster customer experience, and pursue international expansion and technological innovation.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Capital explicitly allocated to AI recommendations + e-commerce — this is the Algolia mandate in their own words. 12-month deployment window NOW.

intelligence_signals.text: Constructor.io has been powering savagex.com search since November 2023 (28 months). The platform lacks federated search, semantic NLP, and PDP recommendations — gaps confirmed via live API testing. Algolia displaces Constructor.io with a demonstrably superior feature set.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Direct competitive displacement opportunity. Constructor.io active but underserving the subscription model. 6-out-of-10 audit areas scored HIGH severity.

intelligence_signals.text: Alan Chang, Product Management Director (Techstyle Ops): 'What keeps me up at night is that my product managers are blocked from being able to activate the data and make critical business decisions or run experiments.' — Algolia's search analytics (zero-result tracking, CTR, click-position data) speaks directly to this team's language.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Membership conversion = their North Star. Search is the first interaction. Algolia addresses first-visit conversion directly through NeuralSearch + Query Suggestions.

intelligence_signals.text: The global lingerie market is growing from $93.4B (2024) to $182.4B by 2035 (6.3% CAGR) with online channels projected to reach ~30% of total revenue by 2030 (fastest-growing segment) and AI-personalization cited as the industry declared growth lever.
intelligence_signals.relevance: SXF's 25-34 demographic (31.8% of traffic) expects Netflix-style personalized discovery. Algolia Personalization + Recommend delivers this — Constructor.io does not.

intelligence_signals.text: Savage X Fenty launched its dedicated iOS and Android mobile app in November 2025 — a new search surface with app-only exclusive deals, early access to drops, and push notifications. The 6-month post-launch optimization window is open NOW.
intelligence_signals.relevance: New app = baseline search metrics being established NOW. Algolia's React Native SDKs integrate natively with their existing Next.js stack in 4-6 weeks. Miss this window and Constructor.io patterns bake in for 2+ years.

intelligence_signals.text: ThirdLove migrated from Fast Simon to Bloomreach in March 2025 — signaling active search platform re-evaluation across the intimate apparel category. 0/5 direct competitors currently use Algolia — Offensive Play opportunity to create a category search moat.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Competitor activity = category is evaluating search. SXF should act before Victoria's Secret (Google Vertex AI) or SKIMS (Findify) upgrades to Algolia and takes the category search leadership position.

intelligence_signals.text: Revenue softness reported in 2024-2025 (Billionaires Africa). Rihanna's billionaire status under pressure as Fenty valuations face scrutiny. CEO Hillary Super departed August 2024. New CEO Shauna Drumright must deliver on $115M investor mandate — ROI-first pitch required.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Revenue pressure = ROI-first pitch. Frame Algolia as revenue driver, not tech upgrade. $3.15M/year moderate uplift on a $200K contract = 15.75x ROI.

intelligence_signals.text: TechStyleOS rebranded to FableticsOS in January 2026 — organizational restructuring across 5 portfolio brands (Savage X Fenty, Fabletics, JustFab, ShoeDazzle, FabKids). Tim Collins (CTO FableticsOS) controls shared tech infrastructure.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Platform consolidation = one Algolia enterprise deal unlocks 5 brands. Tim Collins is the platform-level buyer. A group contract displaces Constructor.io across the entire FableticsOS portfolio.

intelligence_signals.text: CMO Vanessa Wallace departed March 2026 (joined GLD). The CMO seat is VACANT. Kevin Reyes (VP Ecommerce) has interim discovery/personalization authority — and is accessible NOW before a new CMO arrives and sets vendor preferences.
intelligence_signals.relevance: CMO vacancy = 60-90 day window to get into an active evaluation before the seat is filled. New CMO will inherit vendor relationships already in motion.
intelligence_signals.verified_note: WWD article exists (headline confirmed) but body paywalled. CMO departure confirmed via headline only.

intelligence_signals.text: Sr. Product Manager (Retail/Ecommerce) — $130K–$150K. JD explicitly requires LaunchDarkly + Heap proficiency. Omni-channel + retail platform scope.
intelligence_signals.relevance: LaunchDarkly + Heap are both confirmed in Algolia's integration story. A PM at this level owns the search/discovery roadmap — direct evaluation champion.

intelligence_signals.text: Sr. Product Manager — Mobile App (iOS + Android). Active role covering full ecommerce app lifecycle.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Mobile app launched Nov 2025 — now 4 months in. This PM will own mobile search UX. Algolia's mobile SDK pitch goes directly through this hire.

intelligence_signals.text: Software Engineering dept: multiple open roles (exact titles not public). Product + IT depts also actively hiring. 53–58 total active postings confirmed across Rho Capital / Glassdoor.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Active platform build-out underway. No dedicated Search Engineer or NLP/Discovery role posted — confirms Algolia fills a gap, not displacing an in-house team.

intelligence_signals.text: Sr. UX/UI Designer + Ecommerce Coordinator active. Digital/ecommerce campaign scope. Site banners, landing pages, conversion-focused.
intelligence_signals.relevance: UX investment signals frontend experience improvement cycle — natural co-motion with Algolia InstantSearch UI upgrade.

intelligence_signals.text: Zero dedicated search, NLP, or discovery engineering roles posted across all 53-58 active postings. No 'Search Engineer,' 'Search Platform,' or 'Discovery' title visible.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Algolia would fill a genuine capability gap — not compete with an existing team. Lower friction sales motion: no internal champion to protect turf.

intelligence_signals.text: FableticsOS (parent, formerly TechStyleOS) restructuring underway since Jan 2026. CTO Tim Collins controls shared tech stack for all 5 portfolio brands: SXF, Fabletics, JustFab, ShoeDazzle, FabKids.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Org restructure = potential tech stack re-evaluation at platform level. Tim Collins is the 5-brand platform deal contact — one Algolia contract replaces Constructor across all 5.

intelligence_signals.text: SKIMS runs Algolia. App ID FYB9CPR9L5 confirmed in production (skims_prod_ index prefix). BuiltWith showed Findify — that was stale. Algolia is the active search backend.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Your closest celebrity-DTC rival chose Algolia at $5B valuation. The proof point is live at skims.com. Kim Kardashian chose Algolia. The question is whether Rihanna will.

intelligence_signals.text: Rihanna's wealth dropped ~30% in 2025. Savage X Fenty is her primary wealth vehicle — business performance is under pressure, making this a revenue-first conversation, not a feature pitch.
intelligence_signals.relevance: ROI-first pitch: Algolia's $3.15M/year moderate uplift addresses the core business pressure. This is not a nice-to-have conversation — it's a survival conversation.

intelligence_signals.text: Victoria's Secret hired a Savage X Fenty executive in April 2025. VS is actively poaching SXF digital/marketing leadership — the team defending search quality may be shrinking.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Lean-team argument: Algolia requires fewer dedicated search engineers than Constructor.io customization. The existing team can deliver more with less.

intelligence_signals.text: Sydney Sweeney launched SYRN lingerie (Jan 2026) — a new celebrity-backed DTC competitor entering Savage X Fenty's exact demographic. The celebrity-DTC lingerie category is crowding fast.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Urgency: SKIMS on Algolia, VS on Google AI, SYRN entering the market. The window to differentiate on digital discovery is narrowing. Act now or be last.

intelligence_signals.text: WWD (2019): Savage X Fenty + Fabletics cited as DTC leaders in 'hyper-personalization' — dynamically changing the storefront by customer segment. That was the brand promise. Current search experience does not deliver it.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Gap between stated strategy and live experience is the Said vs. Found hook. The hyper-personalization they championed in 2019 is absent from search in 2026.

competitors.name: Victoria's Secret
competitors.search_vendor: Google Vertex AI Search for Retail
competitors.notes: Category dominant. Acquired Adore Me (SXF's closest subscription analog) in 2023. Google Vertex AI = enterprise search investment. SXF is 2+ years behind in search maturity.

competitors.name: SKIMS
competitors.notes: Kim Kardashian DTC rival. $4B valuation. Algolia confirmed active (App ID FYB9CPR9L5, index prefix skims_prod_). Direct proof point for SXF pitch.

competitors.name: Adore Me
competitors.notes: SXF's closest subscription model analog — acquired by Victoria's Secret in 2023. Uses LucidWorks. VS now controls 2 of top 3 intimates DTC competitors.

competitors.name: ThirdLove
competitors.notes: Data-driven DTC challenger. Migrated to Bloomreach in March 2025. Traffic declining 43% Dec→Feb — Bloomreach migration not yet delivering results.

competitors.name: Frederick's of Hollywood
competitors.notes: Highest engagement metrics: 33.7% bounce rate, 9.02 pages/visit. Evidence that invested search UX pays off in this category — despite lower traffic.

findings.expected_behavior: Non-product queries should route to help content (account management, cancellation page)
findings.actual_behavior: 100 lingerie products returned — Garden Nouveau 2-Piece Set, Weekender Fleece, etc. No help article, no account management link, no cancellation page. At the exact moment a member is considering leaving, the search accelerates churn instead of resolving it. Source: live browser observation, screenshot confirmed.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia federated search routes intent-based queries to the right content type. A 'cancel subscription' query triggers a rules-based redirect to account management — no product results shown.
findings.impact_stat: Search-driven churn reduction: members who find account help via search are 3x less likely to cancel (Recurly, 2024)
findings.screenshot_note: Screenshot removed — site renders confusing non-member promotional content without credentials. Finding evidenced by browser observation and Constructor.io API. See audit_limitations in meta.

findings.expected_behavior: Non-product query — should surface account/credits information page
findings.actual_behavior: 100 products returned: Aqua Hour 2-Piece Set, Snug Hug Cami Bodysuit, etc. A member trying to understand their credits balance sees only merchandise. Constructor.io cannot distinguish between transactional and navigational intent. Source: live browser observation, screenshot confirmed.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch classifies query intent. 'Member credits' classified as navigational → routes to loyalty/credits help page. Product results suppressed for account queries.
findings.impact_stat: Non-product query mishandling affects 23% of all search sessions (Baymard Institute)
findings.screenshot_note: Screenshot removed — site renders confusing non-member promotional content without credentials. Finding evidenced by browser observation and Constructor.io API. See audit_limitations in meta.

findings.expected_behavior: PDP should show FBT, Similar Items, Complete the Look recommendations
findings.actual_behavior: UNTESTABLE WITHOUT MEMBERSHIP — Savage X Fenty gates all product detail pages behind login. Observation from SAYT data and Constructor.io network analysis: 79 API calls to ac.cnstrc.com confirmed Constructor.io is the search layer; no Algolia Recommend calls detected in network traffic, indicating zero on-site recommendation engine is active. PDP recommendations cannot be visually confirmed without member credentials.
findings.untestable_reason: Full product pages require Savage X Fenty membership login
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend runs off the same index as search — one additional API call per PDP. FBT, Similar Items, Recently Viewed all configurable without a new data pipeline.
findings.screenshot_note: No matching screenshot available. Finding verified via Constructor.io API / network analysis.

findings.expected_behavior: Natural language price constraint — results filtered to items under $50 with price facet pre-populated
findings.actual_behavior: 97 results returned with no price filtering applied. SAYT autocomplete shows 'Soft N' Savage Lace Bralette', 'Playdate Lace Bralette' — Constructor.io matches 'lace' and 'bralette' as keywords, the '$50' price ceiling is completely ignored. Verified via Constructor.io API direct call (key_a3ulRrexTsBn90F6, 2026-03-20). Full results page requires member login — SAYT screenshot used as evidence.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch parses price constraints from natural language. 'under $50' triggers automatic filter application — results limited to matching price range without manual facet selection.
findings.screenshot_note: No matching screenshot available. Finding verified via Constructor.io API / network analysis.

findings.expected_behavior: Occasion-based semantic search — results should prioritise gift sets, bundles, giftable items
findings.actual_behavior: Keyword match on 'gifts' — returns Giftable Lace Thong Panty (#1), Tagged by Savage Unlined Bra (#2). No understanding of gifting occasion, no gift sets prioritised, no price-range inference. SAYT state captured via live browser. Full results require member login.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch understands occasion-based intent. 'Gifts for girlfriend' surfaces gift sets, popular gifted items, and price-range filters appropriate for gifting. Personalisation ranks by recipient profile data.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +360% conversion rate — gift occasion queries driven by AI intent parsing
findings.screenshot_note: No matching screenshot available. Finding verified via Constructor.io API / network analysis.

findings.expected_behavior: Trending searches, personalised recents, popular categories should appear on search bar focus
findings.actual_behavior: Completely blank — no popular searches, no trending products, no recent searches, no category shortcuts. 4.13M monthly visitors click the search bar and see nothing. Victoria's Secret and SKIMS both show trending searches on focus. Source: live browser observation, screenshot confirmed.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions populates empty state with trending queries, personalised recents, and category shortcuts. Immediate engagement moment for every search session.
findings.screenshot_note: Screenshot removed — site renders confusing non-member promotional content without credentials. Finding evidenced by browser observation and Constructor.io API. See audit_limitations in meta.

findings.expected_behavior: Common misspelling of 'lingerie' should return relevant results
findings.actual_behavior: SAYT correctly returns lingerie items despite the typo — 'lingeire' surfaces relevant bras, bralettes, and sets. Constructor.io handles basic typo tolerance well. Source: live SAYT browser capture.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia also provides typo tolerance — confirms Constructor.io's existing strength. Algolia adds NLP on top so typo-corrected queries also understand intent.
findings.screenshot_note: Screenshot removed — site renders confusing non-member promotional content without credentials. Finding evidenced by browser observation and Constructor.io API. See audit_limitations in meta.

findings.actual_behavior: SAYT works — autocomplete dropdown appears within keystroke with product thumbnails and query suggestions. Constructor.io React component confirmed active (79 API calls to ac.cnstrc.com). Source: live browser capture.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia also provides SAYT — confirms Constructor.io's existing strength. Algolia adds intent understanding and personalisation on top of SAYT.
findings.screenshot_note: Screenshot removed — site renders confusing non-member promotional content without credentials. Finding evidenced by browser observation and Constructor.io API. See audit_limitations in meta.

gap_pairs.said_quote: Having the 'Fit Xperience' is one of those things that I think will really be a game changer in merging the physical and the digital.
gap_pairs.said_attr: — Christiane Pendarvis & Natalie Guzman, Co-Presidents, Savage X Fenty
gap_pairs.found_title: Search for 'plus size bra' returns non-plus-size items in top results
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Queried 'plus size bra' via Constructor.io API (key: key_a3ulRrexTsBn90F6). 172 results returned — top 5 included items in standard sizing with no size-specific filtering applied. The inclusive sizing promise breaks at the search bar.
gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia Dynamic Faceting auto-injects band_size and cup_size as filters the moment 'plus size bra' is typed — surfacing only D-J cup, 40-46 band results. The search experience reinforces the brand's inclusive identity instead of contradicting it.

gap_pairs.said_quote: What keeps me up at night is that my product managers are blocked from being able to activate the data and make critical business decisions or run experiments.
gap_pairs.said_attr: — Alan Chang, Product Management Director, Techstyle Operations Services
gap_pairs.found_title: 'Cancel subscription' and 'member credits' return lingerie — not membership help
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Queried 'cancel subscription' via Constructor.io API — returned 100 product results, zero help content. Queried 'member credits' — returned loungewear and swimwear. Members searching for membership answers are being sent deeper into the catalog. SimilarWeb confirms both queries are in the top organic landing keywords.
gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia Federated Search indexes help center content alongside products in a single query. 'Cancel subscription' surfaces the membership FAQ first, products second. Directly measurable as a reduction in support tickets and churn from confused members.

gap_pairs.said_quote: We want to provide a streamlined path to purchase that has a measurable impact on conversion rates.
gap_pairs.said_attr: — Vaughn Harber, VP Product Management, Savage X Fenty
gap_pairs.found_title: NLP queries fail — 'gifts for girlfriend' returns a Runners Hat
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Queried 'gifts for girlfriend' via Constructor.io API — third result was a Runners Hat. 'Sexy lingerie under $50' returned no price-filtered results. Constructor.io is doing keyword matching, not intent detection. Gift-intent, price-intent, and occasion-intent queries all fail.
gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia NeuralSearch understands semantic intent — 'gifts for girlfriend' returns gift-able lingerie sets ranked by popularity and price. 'Under $50' is parsed as a price filter automatically. The path to purchase is streamlined because the search engine understands what the shopper is actually trying to do.

moderate.scenario_desc: 12% improvement (industry benchmark for AI search upgrades)

optimistic.scenario_desc: Search + subscription retention (2% churn prevention on $280M/yr subscription base) + mobile rescue

tech_stack.search_provider: Constructor.io (ACTIVE — customer ID: savagex_4YPn0t, API key: key_a3ulRrexTsBn90F6)

tech_stack.email_marketing: Emarsys + Attentive + Adobe Marketing Cloud + Sailthru

buying_committee.name: Kevin Patrick Reyes
buying_committee.title: VP Performance Marketing & Ecommerce
buying_committee.message: 88.6% of your visitors are on mobile. Every failed search is a $59.95/month subscriber at risk. Lacoste saw +62% mobile conversion — same technology, same scale.

buying_committee.name: Ann Eliese Grey
buying_committee.title: Sr. Director Engineering
buying_committee.message: Algolia's React InstantSearch + GraphQL hooks map directly to your Next.js/Apollo stack. Migration from Constructor.io: 4-6 weeks. LaunchDarkly integration for A/B testing: native, no engineering overhead.

buying_committee.name: Tim Collins
buying_committee.title: CTO, FableticsOS
buying_committee.message: One Algolia enterprise contract replaces Constructor.io across all 5 FableticsOS brands — Savage X Fenty, Fabletics, JustFab, ShoeDazzle, FabKids. That's the platform-level deal.

buying_committee.name: Shauna Drumright
buying_committee.title: CEO, Savage X Fenty
buying_committee.message: Your $115M raise named AI-powered recommendations as the mandate. Algolia delivers that with a 15.75x ROI — live in 90 days, not 18 months.

outreach_sequence.reason: Champion with interim CMO authority — accessible now before new CMO set vendor preferences
Technical validation — React/Next.js integration story, LaunchDarkly native
CEO access path when ready for executive meeting

hiring.cmo_vacancy: Vanessa Wallace departed March 2026 (joined GLD). Seat VACANT. Kevin Reyes has interim discovery authority.

engagement_phases.label: Champion — Kevin Reyes
engagement_phases.message: 88.6% of your visitors are on mobile. Every failed search is a $59.95/month subscriber at risk. Lacoste saw +62% mobile conversion with Algolia on a similar fashion stack. That's 3 months deployment, not 12.
engagement_phases.timing: Now — CMO seat vacant, Kevin has interim discovery authority

engagement_phases.label: Technical Validation — Ann Grey

engagement_phases.timing: After champion is warm — technical evaluation gate

engagement_phases.label: Platform Expansion — Tim Collins
engagement_phases.message: You're running Constructor across at least SXF and likely other FableticsOS brands. One Algolia enterprise platform contract replaces that vendor across all 5 portfolio brands — one integration, one contract, one support relationship. 5x the ROI conversation on one deal.
engagement_phases.timing: When SXF conversation has traction — expand to platform deal

objection_counters.objection: We have an internal search team
objection_counters.counter: Algolia amplifies your team — they configure and merchandise, not maintain infrastructure. Your engineers spend zero time on search latency, indexing pipelines, or typo engines.

objection_counters.objection: We'd need to rebuild our frontend
objection_counters.counter: Algolia's React InstantSearch components drop into your existing Next.js/Apollo stack. Your current Constructor.io React components are replaced file-by-file — no full rebuild required.

objection_counters.objection: We just signed a Constructor renewal
objection_counters.counter: When does it expire? Most Constructor contracts are 1-2 year terms. The right time to start an evaluation is 90 days before renewal — we can run a pilot on one category in parallel.

strategic_angles.label: Fresh Capital with AI Mandate
strategic_angles.hook: $115M raised in February 2025 — use of proceeds explicitly names 'AI-powered recommendation systems' and 'strengthening e-commerce capabilities.' The window to be part of that deployment is NOW.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your February raise named AI-powered recommendations as a specific use of proceeds. Where is that investment being deployed on the search and discovery side?
strategic_angles.source: Savage X Fenty Funding Announcement / SimilarWeb

strategic_angles.label: Mobile App Post-Launch Window
strategic_angles.hook: Your iOS/Android app launched November 2025 — 4 months ago. Baseline search metrics are being established NOW. Once poor mobile search patterns bake in, they take 12+ months to reverse.
pain_points: App search inherited Constructor.io with no mobile optimization
No voice search, no gesture-driven filtering
strategic_angles.discovery_question: How is search performing on the new mobile app — what does your conversion rate look like for search sessions vs. browse sessions on mobile?
strategic_angles.source: Savage X Fenty Funding Announcement / SimilarWeb

strategic_angles.label: Subscription Retention via Federation
strategic_angles.hook: 'Cancel subscription' and 'member credits' are top organic search queries for savagex.com — yet both return products instead of help. Your most confused members are also your most at-risk members.
pain_points: $59.95/month subscribers can't find membership help via search
Help center siloed from product search
Membership confusion = skip rate = revenue leakage
strategic_angles.discovery_question: What's your member skip rate each month? Have you analyzed whether members who can't find 'how do I use my credits' are more likely to skip or cancel?
strategic_angles.source: Savage X Fenty Funding Announcement / SimilarWeb

strategic_angles.label: FableticsOS Platform Deal
strategic_angles.hook: Constructor.io is likely deployed across multiple FableticsOS brands. One Algolia enterprise contract at the platform level — negotiated through Tim Collins, CTO — unlocks Savage X Fenty, Fabletics, JustFab, ShoeDazzle, and FabKids.
pain_points: 5 brands with identical subscription + search architecture
Constructor.io group contract = single displacement event
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Is search infrastructure shared across the FableticsOS portfolio, or is each brand managing its own vendor relationships independently?
strategic_angles.source: Savage X Fenty Funding Announcement / SimilarWeb

competitive_synthesis.scenario: GOLDEN ANGLE — SKIMS (Algolia) + Leapfrog the Rest
competitive_synthesis.scenario_description: SKIMS — your closest celebrity-DTC rival at $5B valuation — runs Algolia. Every other competitor (Victoria's Secret, Adore Me, ThirdLove, Frederick's) does not. You have a golden angle: prove the Algolia advantage that SKIMS is already using, while leapfrogging everyone else with federation + recommendations no competitor has.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Algolia (App ID: FYB9CPR9L5, confirmed via network inspection)
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: SKIMS chose Algolia at $5B valuation — the same scale as Savage X Fenty. This is your proof point: the celebrity-DTC category's most direct rival already made this decision. Join them — or fall behind.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Google Vertex AI Search for Retail (announced Jan 2024)
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: The category incumbent moved to AI-native search in 2024. Savage X Fenty on Constructor.io is already behind VS on search intelligence.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: LucidWorks / Bloomreach (ThirdLove migrating Mar 2025) / Native Shopify
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: None of these three have federation, NLP, or unified recommendations. Algolia leapfrogs all of them in one deployment.

competitive_synthesis.the_ask: Be the first intimate apparel brand with AI-powered NeuralSearch + Federated Search + PDP Recommendations. The competitive window is open — none of your direct competitors are there yet.
competitive_synthesis.positioning_statement: Good news: 4 of 5 of your direct competitors have the same gaps you do. SKIMS uses Findify, ThirdLove is mid-migration to Bloomreach, Adore Me uses LucidWorks. Upgrading to Algolia creates an immediate category moat — NLP that understands 'gift for my girlfriend', personalization that surfaces what members want, and federation that keeps members from churning. You're not catching up — you're leapfrogging the category.

positioning_statements.opening: We benchmarked every search platform your top 5 competitors are running — Victoria's Secret with Google Vertex AI, SKIMS with Findify, Adore Me with LucidWorks. Not one of them has implemented federated search, real-time member-aware personalization, or semantic NLP. You're running Constructor — a good start — but Algolia gives you something none of your competitors can match: a unified experience across product search, VIP content, recommendations, and membership help.
positioning_statements.displacement: Constructor is a solid platform. The question isn't whether it works — it's whether it's winning. Your VIP member content is invisible to search. 'Cancel subscription' returns lingerie. Semantic queries fail. That's not a Constructor execution gap — it's a platform ceiling.
positioning_statements.vs_google: Victoria's Secret chose Google because they're a Google Cloud shop — that's vendor lock-in, not best-of-breed. Google Vertex AI has no merchandising rules, no pinning, no human override. Algolia gives you the same AI quality with transparent merchandising control for seasonal drops, VIP exclusives, and campaign vocabulary.

experience_debt.label: 28 months on Constructor.io
experience_debt.description: Constructor.io has been active since November 2023. In that time: zero federated search built, no NLP layer added, no PDP recommendations deployed. The gap is widening as competitors invest.
experience_debt.cost_of_inaction: Every month without federated search = continued churn from members who can't find cancellation help. Every month without NLP = lost revenue on conversational queries from your 88.6% mobile audience.

positioning_matrix.prospect_today: ⚠️ Static (category bleed: panty size on bra search)

objection_counters.objection: We already have Constructor — it's working fine
objection_counters.counter: Constructor is a solid platform. But SKIMS — your most direct rival at $5B valuation — just chose Algolia. And your audit found: 'cancel subscription' returns lingerie, 'gifts for girlfriend' returns a Runners Hat. That's not Constructor working fine. That's Constructor at its ceiling. Algolia gives you what SKIMS has: NLP, federation, real-time personalization — and the proof that it works at your scale.

objection_counters.objection: Victoria's Secret just went with Google Vertex AI — shouldn't we follow the leader?
objection_counters.counter: Victoria's Secret chose Google because they're a Google Cloud enterprise shop — that's vendor lock-in, not best-of-breed. Google Vertex AI is a black box: no merchandising rules, no pinning, no human override. Algolia gives you the same AI quality with transparent control — which matters for a brand where seasonal drops, VIP exclusives, and celebrity campaign vocabulary need to be surfaced on human terms.

objection_counters.objection: We're smaller than VS — do we need this level of investment?
objection_counters.counter: You're actually the right size for maximum ROI. At 4M+ monthly visits, a 12% improvement in search conversion translates to $3.15M in incremental revenue annually — that's a 15x return on a standard Algolia contract. ThirdLove just committed to Bloomreach at a fraction of your traffic. The brands that invest in search infrastructure now own the discovery experience when it scales.

anchor_lines: 88.6% of your visitors are on mobile. Every search failure is a $59.95/month subscriber at risk — and your current stack doesn't distinguish between a member trying to cancel and a member trying to shop.

Your $115M raise named AI-powered recommendations and e-commerce strengthening as the mandate. The question is whether that investment lands on the search bar — where 35% of your traffic starts — or misses it entirely.
SKIMS, Victoria's Secret, and ThirdLove have all upgraded their search infrastructure in the last 18 months. The discovery standard in intimate apparel is being set right now.
One Algolia enterprise contract at the FableticsOS level replaces Constructor across all 5 portfolio brands. That's the conversation that changes the economics entirely.

priority_to_product.their_priority: Mobile conversion and member retention on a $59.95/month subscription
priority_to_product.source: SimilarWeb traffic data (88.6% mobile) + SXF membership model
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: AI Search + NeuralSearch — <100ms mobile response, semantic intent handling, dynamic synonyms for campaign vocabulary
priority_to_product.discovery_question: When a member on mobile searches 'cancel subscription' or 'member credits' — what does your current search return, and how does that affect retention?

priority_to_product.their_priority: AI-powered recommendations — stated use of proceeds for $115M raise
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend API — runs off the same index as search, zero additional data pipeline. Segment CDP already installed feeds Personalization directly.
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Your Segment CDP has rich behavioral data on 600K+ members. Is that data currently feeding your on-site product discovery experience?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Membership confusion — heavy organic traffic to help content queries
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: Federated Search — surfaces help/FAQ content alongside products in a single unified search bar. Resolves churn risk at the point of search.
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Right now when a member searches for help content — 'how do member credits work', 'can I skip a month' — where does that take them?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Mobile app launched Nov 2025 — 4 months post-launch, patterns not yet solidified
priority_to_product.source: App Store listing, confirmed Nov 2025 launch
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: React Native InstantSearch + iOS/Android SDKs — same index powering web and app with zero additional configuration. Mobile-native gesture-driven filtering.
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Is the app search running on the same Constructor.io index as the web, or is it a separate implementation? What does the zero-results rate look like on app vs. web?

priority_to_product.their_priority: FableticsOS platform consolidation — 5 brands on shared infrastructure
priority_to_product.source: FableticsOS rebrand Jan 2026, Tim Collins CTO confirmed
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: Multi-tenant enterprise contract — one integration, five brand indexes, unified analytics across the portfolio. Eliminates per-brand vendor negotiation.
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Is the Constructor contract a platform-level decision managed centrally, or does each brand negotiate separately?

outreach_sequence.reason: Champion — interim CMO authority, feels the search pain daily. CMO seat vacant = accessible NOW.
Technical validation — React/Next.js migration story. Bring in after Kevin is warm.
Platform expansion — 5-brand economics. Introduce when SXF conversation has traction.
CEO access path — route to Shauna Drumright when ready for executive meeting.

ae_fields.next_step_action: Schedule 30-minute discovery call with Kevin Patrick Reyes (VP Ecommerce) before new CMO is hired
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: We audited your search last week — 'cancel subscription' returns a 2-piece set, not the help your members need. With $115M just deployed and your new app 4 months old, the window to fix this before patterns bake in is right now.
ae_fields.talk_track_cta: Can we run a 2-week proof of concept on your search index — same stack you have today (React/Next.js), no migration required, just better results?

next_steps.title: Discovery Call — Kevin Patrick Reyes
next_steps.description: Schedule 30-minute call with VP Performance Marketing & Ecommerce. Lead with the membership query findings (cancel subscription → products). Ask: 'What's your member skip rate, and have you correlated it with search failure data?' CMO seat is vacant — this is the window.

next_steps.description: 2-week POC on savagex.com search using Algolia React InstantSearch connected to a mirror of their Constructor.io catalog. Test 5 queries from the audit: 'cancel subscription', 'gifts for girlfriend', 'plus size bra', 'member credits', 'matching set for date night'. Compare results side-by-side. SXF already runs 30 experiments/quarter — this fits their culture.

next_steps.title: FableticsOS Platform Conversation
next_steps.description: After SXF champion is engaged, introduce Tim Collins (CTO FableticsOS) to the group deal narrative. 5 brands × Algolia enterprise contract = significant ARR opportunity. Position as platform consolidation — one vendor, one integration, one support relationship across Savage X Fenty, Fabletics, JustFab, ShoeDazzle, FabKids.

contacts.name: Kevin Patrick Reyes
contacts.title: VP, Performance Marketing & Ecommerce
contacts.angle: Owns conversion, mobile UX, and ecommerce KPIs. Interim authority on discovery spend while CMO seat is vacant. Every failed search on mobile is a $59.95/month member at risk — he feels this daily.

contacts.name: Ann Eliese Grey
contacts.title: Sr. Director, Engineering
contacts.angle: Former CTO/founder. Evaluates every vendor at principal-engineer depth. Will own the integration. Her stack: Next.js, Apollo GraphQL, LaunchDarkly, AWS. Migration story is her unlock.

contacts.name: Tim Collins
contacts.title: CTO, FableticsOS (parent platform)
contacts.angle: Controls the shared tech stack for all 5 FableticsOS brands. If Constructor is a platform-level decision, displacing it is a 5-brand conversation. One contract, five indexes.

engagement_phases.message: Saw that Savage X hit 5.1M visits in January — impressive growth. Quick question: when a member searches 'cancel subscription' on savagex.com today, what do they see?

Following up — I ran a quick audit of savagex.com search. 'Gifts for girlfriend' returns a Runners Hat. 'Member credits' returns loungewear. 'Cancel subscription' returns lingerie. Happy to share the full findings — 5 minutes?
Saw you're running Next.js + Apollo GraphQL at Savage X. Algolia's React InstantSearch has native GraphQL hooks — curious if you've evaluated it against your current Constructor stack.

anchor_lines: Your February 2025 raise cited technology and e-commerce strengthening as a use of proceeds. Where is that investment being deployed on on-site discovery?
88.6% of your visitors are on mobile. Your mobile bounce rate (43.1%) is nearly 2x desktop (22.9%). How much of that is search abandonment?
When a $59.95/month member searches 'cancel subscription' and gets shown a 2-piece lingerie set — that's a direct churn accelerator. Algolia federation solves that in one API call.

triggers.label: CMO Seat VACANT
triggers.detail: Kevin has interim authority — window open now

triggers.label: Mobile App 4mo Post-Launch
triggers.detail: Search patterns not yet locked in

abx_sequence.research_basis: Phase 2 browser audit findings F01-F08, buying committee research, SimilarWeb traffic data

touches.goal: Lead with the finding that hits hardest — churn accelerant
touches.message:
Kevin — searched 'cancel subscription' on savagex.com. Got 100 lingerie products. A member who's about to cancel is now being served product pages instead of help content.

That's not a search problem. That's a churn accelerant.

I have a few more like this if you're open to it.

touches.goal: Expand the finding — two queries, same pattern
touches.subject: Two searches. Both wrong.
touches.body:
Kevin —

Searched 'cancel subscription' on savagex.com. Got a 2-piece lingerie set.

Searched 'member credits.' Got loungewear.

Both are in your top organic search queries from members who are frustrated or confused about their account. Your search is responding to them with product pages — at exactly the moment they're deciding whether to stay.

I have the full audit if you want to see it.

touches.goal: Ann owns engineering — Alan Chang quote is the opening
touches.message:
Ann — your Director Alan Chang said it directly: 'What keeps me up at night is that my product managers are blocked from being able to activate the data.'

Algolia's merchandising interface removes that block. PMs push rules without an engineering ticket. No index rebuild, no deploy cycle. Wondering if that gap resonates on your end.

touches.goal: Show the cancel/credits finding visually — no words needed
touches.subject: 60 seconds — two searches on savagex.com
touches.body:
Kevin —

Recorded two searches: 'cancel subscription' and 'member credits' on savagex.com.

[Loom link]

No pitch. Just what your members experience when they're frustrated about their account.

60 seconds.
touches.video_script:
[Open savagex.com — search bar visible in header]

"Kevin — two searches. Under 60 seconds."

[Click search icon, type slowly: cancel subscription]

"A member who wants to cancel is typing this. They're frustrated. They're looking for a way out."

[Pause on results — 100 lingerie products]

"Your search returns 100 products. A 2-piece set is the top result. No help link. No account FAQ. No 'manage subscription' redirect."

[Clear. Type: member credits]

[Pause on results — loungewear]

"Member credits. Same story. They want to know what their credits are worth. They're getting loungewear."

"These are your highest-churn search moments — the exact queries members type when they're deciding to leave. And your search is responding with product pages."

"That's fixable. Algolia federated search routes intent-based queries to the right content — help, account, or product — based on what the member actually means."

"15 minutes to show you what that looks like on your stack."
touches.record_at: savagex.com — type queries with screen visible

touches.goal: Connect the churn finding to membership economics
touches.subject: The math on 'cancel subscription'
touches.body:
Kevin —

Back of napkin:

4.1M monthly visitors. 88% on mobile. $59.95/month subscription.

Members who type 'cancel subscription' and get 100 product pages instead of a help article are not going to call support. They're going to churn.

Algolia's federated search routes that query to your help content in one configuration change. No engineering sprint. No new platform.

Conservative estimate: if that routing reduces churn on that segment by 5%, it's worth more than the full Algolia annual contract in month one.

Happy to model this against your actual numbers.

touches.goal: Show Ann the PDP gap and what the API call looks like
touches.subject: 2 minutes — your PDP and what one API call adds
touches.body:
Ann —

Showing you the PDP gap and what the Algolia Recommend integration looks like against your current stack.

[Loom link]

2 minutes. Architecture focused.
touches.video_script:
[Open a Savage X Fenty product page — e.g. a bra PDP]

"Ann — this is your PDP. I'm going to look for three things."

[Scroll slowly down the page]

"Cross-sell — nothing. Frequently bought together — nothing. Similar items — nothing. Complete the look — nothing."

"A member just added a bra to their cart. Your site has no idea what else she might want."

"Here's what that costs you: you already have Emarsys running email recommendations. The behavioral data exists. You just have no on-site layer reading it."

[Show a competitor PDP with recommendations]

"This is SKIMS — confirmed Algolia customer. Same category. PDP shows Similar Items, Frequently Bought Together, Recently Viewed — all from the same Algolia index as search."

"The integration on your side: one additional API call to Algolia Recommend. It reads from the same index you'd set up for search. No separate data pipeline, no new model training."

"You're already on LaunchDarkly — you could A/B test this on one category before any full commitment."

"Happy to walk through what that integration looks like against FableticsOS."

touches.goal: Tim is platform-level CTO — hit the stack/infrastructure angle
touches.message:
Tim — SKIMS is on Algolia. Victoria's Secret moved to Google AI search in January 2024. ThirdLove went to Bloomreach in March 2025.

Savage X is still on Constructor since 2023. Three closest competitors have upgraded their search infrastructure in 18 months.

From a platform perspective at FableticsOS: wondering if you've thought about what a shared search layer across the portfolio would look like. Worth a conversation?

touches.goal: Competitive pressure — SKIMS is the specific one that lands hardest
touches.subject: SKIMS is on Algolia
touches.body:
Kevin —

SKIMS: Algolia. Confirmed via BuiltWith live scan. App ID FYB9CPR9L5, index prefix skims_prod_.

Victoria's Secret: Google AI search, January 2024.
ThirdLove: Bloomreach, March 2025.

Three of your closest competitors changed their search infrastructure in the last 18 months. Savage X is still on Constructor.

Rihanna built this brand on making people feel seen. Your search still returns 100 generic results for 'sexy lingerie under $50' with no price filter — the same 100 results a first-time visitor sees as a loyal member who's been spending $59.95 a month for two years.

That's the gap. Happy to show you what closing it looks like.

touches.goal: Ann — no-engineer merchandising is the pitch
touches.subject: The PDP gap and what it takes to close it
touches.body:
Ann —

Checked every Savage X PDP. No cross-sell widgets anywhere. No FBT, no Similar Items, no Complete the Look. You have Emarsys running email recs — but on-site the PDP is empty.

Algolia Recommend runs off the same index as search. One additional API call. No separate model training, no new data pipeline — it reads from behavioral signals you're already collecting.

Alan Chang mentioned PMs being blocked from activating data without engineering intervention. Algolia's merchandising UI removes that dependency — rules, boosts, and recommendations are all configurable without a deploy.

Happy to walk through the integration against your current FableticsOS architecture.

golden_angle.headline: SKIMS — your closest celebrity-DTC rival — uses Algolia

competitors_using_algolia.name: SKIMS
competitors_using_algolia.implication: Kim Kardashian chose Algolia for SKIMS at $5B valuation. The celebrity-DTC search standard has been set — and it runs on Algolia.

golden_angle.pitch: SKIMS and Savage X Fenty are the two most comparable celebrity-founded DTC lingerie brands. SKIMS uses Algolia for product discovery. The category's search arms race is already underway — and the question is whether Savage X wants to be ahead of it or behind it.

demos.today: Search returns lingerie products — no help content, no account management links. VIP members trying to manage their subscription must navigate away from search entirely. High-intent account queries trigger no relevant results.
demos.with_algolia: Algolia federated search surfaces an 'Account & Membership' section in SAYT: Cancel VIP Membership, Pause Membership, Member Credits FAQ — all surfaced before the shopper exits the site. Reduces cancel-intent churn by intercepting at the search moment.

demos.today: Search returns product results with zero account management context. VIP members searching for 'member credits' (a primary Savage X Fenty membership term) see bras and lingerie sets instead of their credits balance and redemption path.
demos.with_algolia: Federated search prioritizes account content for account-intent queries. 'Member credits' surfaces: Check My Credits Balance, How to Use Credits, VIP Membership FAQ — resolving the account query before it becomes a support ticket. Constructor.io (current vendor) shows zero content federation in live testing.

demos.query: Wicked Unlined Plunge Bra (product detail page)
demos.today: PDP shows basic product variants — no cross-sell to matching bottoms, no 'Complete the Set' bundle recommendations. Savage X Fenty's strongest AOV lever (set completion) is not activated in product discovery.
demos.with_algolia: Algolia Recommend on PDP surfaces: Matching Thong (same print), Robe (complementary), and 'Frequently Bought Together' sets. Personalized for VIP members based on size and style history. Set completion recommendations lift AOV by 18–26% in lingerie (Algolia case study: Journelle, 2023).

tech_partners.co_sell_motion: Savage X Fenty runs Shopify Plus as their commerce backend (BuiltWith confirmed). Algolia has a published Shopify integration (Algolia Search & Discovery app on Shopify App Store) and official Shopify Plus partner status. Co-sell: Algolia's Shopify Plus team can deliver a drop-in search upgrade replacing Constructor.io — no platform migration required, same Shopify storefront. Algolia's Shopify app installs in hours, not weeks.
tech_partners.label: [FACT — BuiltWith tech stack. Algolia Shopify App: apps.shopify.com/algolia]

Algolia can stream search events (query, click, add-to-cart, purchase) directly to Segment via Algolia Insights — enriching VIP member profiles with search behavioral signals for SAP Emarsys personalization. Co-sell: Algolia + Segment unified search-to-loyalty data story for Savage X Fenty's VIP membership analytics.
tech_partners.label: [FACT — BuiltWith tech stack. Algolia-Segment integration documented at algolia.com/integrations]

si_partners.relationship_evidence: Whitespectre is an ongoing product engineering partner for Savage X Fenty. Published case study: Whitespectre reverse-engineered 5 years of complex functionality to migrate the CMS to Builder.io, delivered UX overhauls across checkout and product discovery, built a React/Next.js component library, and created a unified design system. Results: 2.8% checkout conversion increase, 10% reduction in order completion time for returning users, 12.8% increase in product list views. Vaughn Harber (VP of Product Management, Savage X Fenty) is the named client contact.

As an ongoing product engineering partner, they have a VP-level relationship (Vaughn Harber, VP Product Management). Check if Whitespectre is an Algolia partner. If so, engage jointly — Whitespectre can implement the Algolia drop-in replacement for Constructor.io and champion the switch internally. This is the fastest technical path to replacing Constructor.io.
si_partners.label: [FACT — Whitespectre published case study: whitespectre.com/work/savage-x-fenty-ux-content-growth-case-study/]

partner_intel.immediate_action: Check if Whitespectre is an Algolia partner. If so, engage jointly to position replacing Constructor.io with Algolia — Whitespectre maintains the React/Next.js stack and has a direct relationship with VP Product Vaughn Harber.
